item.title: 纽约证券交易所将向美国证券交易委员会支付900万美元罚款，该处罚与2023年市场停摆事件引发的动荡有关。
item.description: 纽约证券交易所同意支付900万美元，以解决美国证券交易委员会的指控。该指控称，2023年1月某日，由于内部故障导致市场开盘混乱，引发单日股价剧烈波动。【原文：The New York Stock Exchange agreed to pay a $9 million to settle US Securities and Exchange Commission allegations that an internal malfunction led to a botched market open and wild price swings on a single day in January 2023.】

item.title: SpaceX持股激增引发对ETF中私有资产的质疑
item.description: SpaceX的一笔小额投资，让这家小众基金在近几个月间声名鹊起，如今已演变为巨额持仓，正考验着交易所交易基金持有非上市资产的能力极限。【原文：A modest investment in SpaceX that thrust a niche fund into the limelight in recent months has morphed into a monster position, testing the very capacity of exchange-traded funds to hold unlisted assets.】

item.title: 铝供应冲击引发大规模期权押注进入实值状态
item.description: 本周，随着伊朗战争引发铝价暴涨，一笔巨额铝期权交易进入实值状态，为交易商带来意外之财——上周该头寸的出现曾令交易商们大感震惊。【原文：A huge aluminum options trade has come into the money this week as the war in Iran sparked a sharp rally in prices, delivering a windfall after the appearance of the position stunned dealers last week.】

item.title: 美元创2024年以来最佳单周表现，油价飙升削弱美联储加息预期
item.description: 美元正迎来逾一年来最强劲的一周，在中东冲突和油价飙升的背景下，作为终极避险资产强势反弹。【原文：The dollar is wrapping up its best week in more than a year, rallying as the ultimate safe haven amid the conflict in the Middle East and skyrocketing oil prices.】

item.title: 三星Galaxy S26 Ultra：以隐私为先的性能怪兽
item.description: 要使最新款智能手机成为前代产品的实质性升级，并不需要彻底重塑设计。尽管苹果公司去年为其Pro系列采用了大胆的新外观设计，并自此持续受到市场热捧，但三星电子在Galaxy S26 Ultra上并未进行类似的视觉革新。【原文：You don’t have to completely redesign a smartphone to make the latest version a solid upgrade over its predecessor. While Apple Inc. went with a bold new look for its Pro models last year and has enjoyed strong demand ever since, Samsung Electronics Co. refrained from a similar visual shakeup for its Galaxy S26 Ultra.】

item.title: 德州AI"男子营地"以高尔夫和免费牛排吸引工人
item.description:
随着数据中心蓬勃发展，开发商正争相在偏远地区兴建住宅和配套设施。【原文：

As data centers boom, developers are rushing to build housing and amenities in remote locales

】

item.title: 欧洲原油价格飙升，亚洲买盘令市场趋紧
item.description: 欧洲原油价格——最初对中东战争反应平淡——如今正因亚洲炼油厂的激进采购导致全球市场趋紧而大幅上涨。【原文：Crude prices in Europe — which initially showed a muted reaction to the war in the Middle East — are now surging as aggressive buying by Asian refiners tightens the global market.】

item.title: 哥伦比亚地方债务抛售潮因油价飙升转为反弹
item.description: 伊朗战争推高油价，本周引发哥伦比亚本地债务强劲反弹。随着该国主要出口商品价格飙升逾20%，其债券表现从新兴市场垫底一跃成为涨幅领头羊。【原文：Higher oil prices driven by the Iran war sparked a rally in Colombia’s local debt this week, converting it from the worst performing in emerging markets to the leading gainer as the price of its main export surged more than 20%.】

item.title: 交易撮合者认为伊朗战争阻碍并购进程，延误尽职调查
item.description: 交易撮合者连续第二年面临这样的局面：他们原本有望创纪录的并购交易额，可能在第一季度结束前就化为泡影。【原文：Dealmakers are, for the second year in a row, seeing their chances of a record haul of mergers and acquisitions potentially fizzle out before the end of the first quarter.】

item.title: IG4告知银行可能放弃与巴西化工巨头Braskem的谈判，Valor报道
item.description: 据《经济价值报》报道，资产管理公司IG4 Capital表示，由于巴西反垄断监管机构推迟对该交易的分析，该公司可能放弃收购巴西石化巨头Braskem SA控股权的谈判。【原文：Asset manager IG4 Capital signaled it may abandon talks to acquire a controlling stake in Brazilian petrochemical giant Braskem SA as the nation’s antitrust watchdog delays its analysis on the deal, according to newspaper Valor Economico.】

item.title: 以下是美国2月就业报告的关键要点
item.description: 以下是周五发布的2月美国就业报告中的关键要点：【原文：Here are the key takeaways from the February US employment report, released Friday:】

item.title: 卡塔尔似乎已装载首船液化天然气，此前该公司曾援引不可抗力条款
item.description: 卡塔尔似乎已装运了首批液化天然气，此前中东地区冲突扩大迫使其暂停燃料生产，并向买家宣布了前所未有的不可抗力。【原文：Qatar appears to have loaded its first liquefied natural gas cargo after the widening conflict in the Middle East forced it to halt fuel production and declare an unprecedented force majeure to buyers.】

item.title: 美国铝买家寻求替代品，伊朗战争颠覆全球供应链
item.description: 随着对伊朗的战争扰乱了主要海外供应源，美国铝买家正争相从亚洲确保替代供应——这一发展可能推高用于汽车零部件、家电和饮料罐的金属成本。【原文：Aluminum buyers in the US are rushing to secure alternative supplies from Asia as the war on Iran disrupts a major foreign source — a development that threatens to hike the cost of the metal used in auto parts, appliances and beverage cans.】

item.title: 美联储达利称劳动力市场疲软表明双重风险持续存在
item.description: 旧金山联邦储备银行行长玛丽·戴利表示，令人失望的2月就业报告动摇了美国劳动力市场正在趋稳的观点。【原文：Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco President Mary Daly said a disappointing February employment report undermines the notion that the US labor market was stabilizing.】

item.title: 金融大鳄巴雷拉斯力图重振投资者对哥伦比亚的信心
item.description: 哥伦比亚总统候选人罗伊·巴雷拉斯指出，该国正面临被鼓吹激进极端政策的民粹主义者所蛊惑的风险。【原文：Colombia is at risk of being seduced by populists espousing radical and extreme policies, according to presidential candidate Roy Barreras.】

item.title: 蓝猫公司对一家倒闭的英国贷款机构有3600万英镑的风险敞口
item.description: 蓝猫资本公司对总部位于伦敦的房地产贷款机构世纪资本合伙有限公司存在3600万英镑（4800万美元）的风险敞口，该机构上月已申请进入破产管理程序。【原文：Blue Owl Capital Inc. has a £36 million ($48 million) exposure to Century Capital Partners Ltd., a London-based property lender that filed for administration last month.】

item.title: 以色列称已摧毁伊朗大部分导弹发射装置
item.description: 以色列国防军表示，已摧毁伊朗大部分弹道导弹发射装置，导致本周瞄准以色列及该地区其他国家的导弹数量持续下降。【原文：The Israel Defense Forces says it’s demolished a majority of Iran’s ballistic-missile launchers, causing the number of missiles targeting Israel and other countries in the region to fall throughout the week.】

item.title: 就业报告出炉后，债券交易员加大押注美联储今年降息力度
item.description: 在就业报告令人失望后，债券投资者加大了押注美联储今年晚些时候降息的力度，尽管能源价格上涨可能助长通胀。【原文：Bond investors added to wagers that the Federal Reserve would lower interest rates later this year after a disappointing employment report, even as a rise in energy prices threatens to fuel inflation.】

item.title: 美国意外减少9.2万个就业岗位，失业率上升
item.description: 美国雇主在2月份意外裁员，失业率上升，这表明劳动力市场仍存在脆弱性，而此前人们认为该市场正在趋于稳定。【原文：US employers unexpectedly cut jobs in February and the unemployment rate rose, pointing to lingering fragility in a labor market that was thought to be stabilizing.】

item.title: 国际能源署称中东危机期间暂无需动用石油储备
item.description: 国际能源署表示，鉴于全球石油供应充足且影响似乎只是暂时的，目前尚无必要在中东危机期间动用紧急石油储备。【原文：The International Energy Agency said it sees no need yet to release emergency oil stockpiles amid the Middle East crisis, as global oil supplies are plentiful and the impact appears likely to be temporary.】

item.title: 期货下跌，油价飙升与战争担忧促使交易员抛售
item.description: 美国股指期货指向下跌，为动荡的一周画上句号。飙升的油价以及中东持久战引发的能源供应冲击阴影，加剧了通胀担忧，并加剧了对经济增长和企业盈利可能遭受冲击的忧虑。【原文：US equity futures pointed to a down day day to end a volatile week, as soaring oil prices and the specter of energy supply shocks from a protracted war in the Middle East fanned inflation fears and heightened concerns about the potential hit to growth and earnings.】

item.title: 黑客冒充以色列国防军向以色列人手机植入间谍软件
item.description: 最新网络安全研究显示，黑客正利用伪装成安全警报应用的间谍软件，对以色列人实施攻击。【原文：Hackers are targeting Israelis with spyware masquerading as a smartphone app that issues safety alerts, according to new cybersecurity research.】

item.title: 议员敦促美国机构限制华为美国子公司
item.description: 美国议员敦促特朗普政府堵住漏洞，这些漏洞曾允许被列入黑名单的中国科技巨头华为技术有限公司旗下子公司自由在美国运营。【原文：US lawmakers urged the Trump administration to close the loopholes that have allowed a unit of blacklisted Chinese tech giant Huawei Technologies Co. to operate freely in the US.】

item.title: 美联储委员沃勒认为伊朗战争不会引发持续通胀
item.description: 美联储理事克里斯托弗·沃勒周五表示，他预计伊朗战争不会对通胀产生持续影响。【原文：Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller Friday said he doesn’t expect the Iran war to have a sustained impact on inflation.】

item.title: 美国盘前活跃股：Gap、Marvell、Nutex Health、英伟达、Samsara
item.description: 截至纽约时间上午7:45，标普500指数期货下跌0.6%，因油价再度攀升，加剧了市场对中东战争助推通胀的担忧。交易员们同时等待周五的就业报告。【原文：S&P 500 Index futures are down 0.6% as of 7:45 a.m. in New York as oil climbs again, fanning concerns that the Middle East war is fueling inflation. Traders also await Friday’s jobs report.】

item.title: 中国批评欧盟"欧洲制造"计划是保护主义
item.description: 中国批评欧盟新近发布的公共采购计划要求成员国优先采购本国企业产品，称此举构成严重投资壁垒和制度性歧视。【原文：China criticized European Union’s newly released plan requiring member states to favor domestic companies in public procurement, saying the move constitutes serious investment barriers and institutional discrimination.】

item.title: 伊朗战争迫使各国央行考虑又一次供应冲击
item.description: 政策制定者们在一点上达成共识：战争对经济增长和通胀的影响，完全取决于战争持续的时间长短。【原文：Policymakers are united on one thing: The impact on growth and inflation will all depend on how long the war lasts.】

item.title: 伊朗战争引发能源出口国争相寻找离开海湾的运输路线
item.description: 中东地区不断扩大的冲突正加剧全球能源危机的担忧，出口商争相寻找离开该地区的运输路线，而大批炼油厂则纷纷减产。【原文：The widening conflict in the Middle East is stoking fears over a global energy crunch as exporters scramble for routes out of the region while a swath of refineries reduce output.】

item.title: 俄罗斯是伊朗战争的首个明确赢家
item.description: 印度炼油商正争相抢购莫斯科的原油，此前中东地区的原油供应因波斯湾局势受阻而陷入停滞。【原文：Indian refiners are snapping up Moscow’s barrels after Middle East flows have bottled up in Persian Gulf.】

item.title: 美国2月非农就业人数料将上升，交易员关注数据修正
item.description: 加利福尼亚州萨克拉门托市一家金属制造厂内，工人们正在打磨金属门。摄影师：David Paul Morris/Bloomberg【原文：Workers sand metal doors at a metal manufacturing facility in Sacramento, California. Photographer: David Paul Morris/Bloomberg】
